Technology.thesis: The AI infrastructure buildout continues to be the dominant driver for the technology sector, with strong demand for chips, data center components, and AI-driven software. While some areas face margin pressure or competitive concerns, the overall trend is toward expanding AI-related investments.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings – would crack the AI demand thesis
Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM – forces a hawkish Fed repricing, killing duration bid for software multiples
China export controls expand to HBM or advanced packaging equipment – significant supply chain disruption

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: Consumer discretionary spending remains a mixed bag. E-commerce and digital services show resilience, but traditional retail faces headwinds from inflation and shifting consumer preferences. Residential construction is under pressure from higher rates.
watchFor: Retail sales data shows significant deceleration (<0.2% MoM)
Mortgage rates break above 8% – further dampens housing demand
Consumer confidence index drops below 90

Communication Services.thesis: The Communication Services sector is navigating the evolving landscape of digital content and advertising, with some players benefiting from shifting consumption patterns while others face competitive pressures and content moderation challenges.
watchFor: Digital advertising spending reports show significant deceleration
Major streaming services report subscriber losses
New regulatory actions on content or AI moderation

Industrials.thesis: The Industrials sector is experiencing divergent trends. Aerospace & Defense benefits from geopolitical tensions, while freight and logistics face uncertainty. Heavy machinery sees mixed signals with some areas booming and others slumping.
watchFor: Significant de-escalation in global geopolitical conflicts
Global trade volumes show sustained decline
ISM Manufacturing PMI drops below 48

Healthcare.thesis: The Healthcare sector is experiencing strong tailwinds from the GLP-1 obesity drug wave and continued innovation in medical devices, despite some regulatory and competitive pressures.
watchFor: New GLP-1 trial data shows significant adverse effects
Major healthcare policy changes impacting drug pricing or reimbursement
FDA rejects key drug or device approvals

Financials.thesis: The Financials sector is showing strength, particularly among diversified banks and credit services, benefiting from regulatory clarity and robust consumer spending. Asset managers are also seeing momentum.
watchFor: Unexpected hawkish shift from the Fed (e.g., rate hike or delayed cuts)
Significant increase in loan defaults or credit card delinquencies
New, stringent banking regulations announced

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The Consumer Defensive sector is showing mixed signals. Discount stores maintain resilience amidst inflationary pressures, while household and personal products face challenges from rising costs and shifting consumer habits.
watchFor: Core CPI drops below 2.5% YoY, reducing pressure on discretionary spending
Significant increase in unemployment rate (>4.5%)
Major retailers report unexpected declines in foot traffic

Utilities.thesis: The Utilities sector, particularly independent power producers, is benefiting from the surging demand for electricity driven by AI data centers, creating a strong growth narrative.
watchFor: Significant slowdown in data center buildouts
New energy efficiency breakthroughs reduce data center power needs
Regulatory caps on electricity prices for large consumers

Basic Materials.thesis: The Basic Materials sector, specifically industrial metals and mining, is influenced by geopolitical dynamics around critical resources, creating a mixed and uncertain outlook.
watchFor: Significant shifts in global trade policies for rare earth elements
New discoveries of critical mineral deposits outside of current dominant regions
Escalation or de-escalation of geopolitical tensions impacting supply chains

Precious Metals.thesis: Precious metals are currently benefiting from geopolitical uncertainty and hopes for a ceasefire, positioning them as safe-haven assets and inflation hedges.
watchFor: Sustained period of global peace and stability
Strong dollar rally (DXY breaks above 105)
Real yields rise significantly (10Y TIPS yield above 2%)
